originalTitle: The hallucinogenic mushroom that contains no known psychedelic
content:
Hundreds of people a year are hospitalised seeing little people after a meal of a prized Yunnan bolete. Its genome has now been read in full, and whatever causes the visions is nothing science recognises.

A globally distributed genus of boletes called Lanmaoa may represent a third family of psychoactive mushrooms, working through a chemistry that no one understands yet.

The genus already holds one of the strangest entries in the toxicological literature. In Yunnan, in southwestern China, eating an undercooked bolete known locally as jian shou qing can bring on vivid hallucinations of tiny people. Patients describe colourful figures only a few centimetres tall, marching, dancing and climbing over the furniture. The effect sharpens with the eyes closed. More than a hundred such cases are treated each year at a single Yunnan hospital, and according to a November 2025 article by the study's lead researcher, Colin Domnauer, published on the University of Utah's news site , 96 percent of those who sought hospital help after eating the mushroom reported seeing little people. The clinical term is Lilliputian hallucination, after the six-inch inhabitants of Gulliver's Travels.

The mushroom responsible for this bizarre phenomenology is Lanmaoa asiatica. Jian shou qing means turns blue in the hand, and it is really a market name for several blue-bruising boletes, but asiatica is the species that carries the documented reputation. It is also a prized edible, which is how people come to eat it undercooked in the first place.

Specimens have been collected across Asia, the Americas and Europe, but the genus, which includes edible species, prized regional delicacies and at least this one notorious hallucinogen, has never had a properly resolved family tree.

Researchers at the University of Utah, writing in Mycologia, have now sequenced whole genomes from 53 specimens and built a phylogeny from 1,515 single-copy orthologous genes. Every major branch in the resulting tree has full statistical support. It is the first time the genus has been mapped at this resolution.

The sampling included 21 type specimens, the original physical references that anchor a species name. Pulling DNA from these has allowed the team to reorganise the genus: six species are renamed into new combinations, four are identified as new to science, and two are formally described here as Lanmaoa fallax and Lanmaoa carbonilivor. The genus now contains 17 recognised species.

The striking result is that Lanmaoa asiatica lacks any known hallucinogenic chemical signature. The team mined its genome and searched for the biosynthetic gene clusters that produce psilocybin in Psilocybe mushrooms and ibotenic acid in Amanita muscaria. Neither was there.

In other studies, chemists have isolated and tested compounds from the fruiting body, or profiled the blood of poisoned patients, yet nothing identified so far accounts for the visions. Taken together, the chemical work and the completed genomic search remove the most familiar explanations and still leave the active agent unaccounted for. Whatever L. asiatica is making, it is doing so via a pathway no one has seen before.

The reports of little people remain consistent and widespread. Tiny figures have been described far from Yunnan, including among communities in the northern Philippines, and a Chinese text from the third century refers to a mushroom that, eaten raw, lets one see "a little person".

Psilocybin and ibotenic acid have shaped almost everything we know about psychoactive fungi: their pharmacology, their cultural histories and, increasingly, their clinical use. A third biosynthetic route, evolved independently in a lineage of boletes more closely related to the common porcini than to any known hallucinogenic species, would widen that picture considerably.
chineseTitle: 致幻蘑菇不含已知致幻剂
chineseContent:
每年都有数百人在吃了珍贵的云南牛肝菌后因看到小人而住院。它的基因组现在已经被完整读取，而造成这种幻象的原因尚不清楚。

分布于全球的牛肝菌属 Lanmaoa 可能是第三类具有精神活性的蘑菇，它通过一种尚无人了解的化学原理发挥作用。

该属已经成为毒理学文献中最奇怪的条目之一。在中国西南部的云南，吃一种当地称为“煎手青”的未煮熟的牛肝菌可以使小人产生生动的幻觉。患者描述了只有几厘米高的彩色人物，他们在家具上行进、跳舞和攀爬。闭上眼睛时效果会更加清晰。每年，云南一家医院都会收治超过一百起此类病例，根据该研究的首席研究员科林·多姆瑙尔 (Colin Domnauer) 2025 年 11 月在犹他大学新闻网站上发表的一篇文章，96% 的食用蘑菇后寻求医院帮助的人表示，看到的人很少。 临床术语是小人国幻觉，以《格列佛游记》中六英寸高的居民命名。

造成这种奇怪现象的蘑菇是兰毛亚洲菌。 “见手青”的意思是手上变成蓝色，这实际上是几种蓝色瘀伤牛肝菌的市场名称，但积雪草是有记录的声誉的物种。它也是一种珍贵的食物，这就是人们最初在未煮熟的情况下食用它的方式。

亚洲、美洲和欧洲都采集到了该属的标本，但该属包括可食用的物种、珍贵的地方美食以及至少这种臭名昭著的致幻剂，但从未有过正确解析的家谱。

犹他大学的研究人员在 Mycologia 上撰文，现已对 53 个样本的整个基因组进行了测序，并根据 1,515 个单拷贝直系同源基因建立了系统发育树。生成的树中的每个主要分支都有完整的统计支持。这是该属首次以这种分辨率绘制图谱。

采样包括 21 个模式标本，这是确定物种名称的原始物理参考。从这些物种中提取 DNA 使得研究小组能够重组该属：六个物种被重新命名为新的组合，四个被确定为科学新物种，两个在这里被正式描述为 Lanmaoafallax 和 Lanmaoacarbonilivor。该属现已包含 17 个公认的物种。

令人震惊的结果是兰毛亚细亚没有任何已知的致幻化学特征。研究小组挖掘了其基因组，并寻找在裸盖菇中产生裸盖菇素和在毒蝇伞中产生鹅膏菌酸的生物合成基因簇。两者都不在那里。

在其他研究中，化学家已经分离并测试了子实体中的化合物，或者分析了中毒患者的血液，但迄今为止还没有发现任何可以解释这些幻象的原因。总而言之，化学工作和完成的基因组搜索消除了最熟悉的解释，但仍然没有找到活性剂。无论 L. asiatica 正在制造什么，它都是通过一种前所未见的途径进行的。

关于小人物的报道仍然一致且广泛。在远离云南的地方，包括菲律宾北部的社区中，也有人描述了微小的人物，而一份来自三世纪的中文文献提到了一种蘑菇，生吃时可以让人看到“一个小人”。

裸盖菇素和鹅膏菌酸塑造了我们对精神活性真菌的几乎所有了解：它们的药理学、它们的文化历史以及越来越多的它们的临床用途。第三种生物合成途径是在牛肝菌谱系中独立进化出来的，与普通牛肝菌的关系比与任何已知的致幻物种的关系更密切，这将大大拓宽这一前景。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
